MSFT.title: Microsoft signs large Azure AI infrastructure agreement with European bank
MSFT.description: Microsoft said a major European bank will expand Azure and Copilot usage across its operations, adding multi-year AI infrastructure spending and broader enterprise deployment.
MSFT.snippet: The agreement expands Azure consumption, Copilot rollout and data platform services, with management pointing to durable enterprise AI demand over the coming quarters.

highlights.highlight: Azure consumption and Copilot adoption remain strong.

MSFT.title: Microsoft says Copilot adoption widens as enterprise customers expand licenses
MSFT.description: Microsoft executives highlighted broader paid Copilot adoption among enterprise clients, with higher attach rates across productivity, cloud and security workflows.
MSFT.snippet: The company said demand remains healthy among large customers that want automation, AI assistants and cloud tooling in one stack.

highlights.highlight: Enterprise customers are expanding paid Copilot licenses.

MSFT.title: Magnificent Seven stocks wobble as traders reassess AI winners
MSFT.description: A broad market roundup reviewed recent weakness across large-cap technology names as investors reassessed valuation and risk appetite.
MSFT.snippet: The article discusses several mega-cap names and broader market tone rather than any one company-specific catalyst.

highlights.highlight: Large-cap technology stocks sold off broadly.

JPM.title: JPMorgan investment banking fees rise as deal pipeline improves
JPM.description: JPMorgan said advisory and underwriting activity improved through quarter-end, with corporate clients returning to capital markets as volatility stabilized.
JPM.snippet: Management indicated stronger fee income and resilient credit performance, offsetting some concern around future rate cuts.

highlights.highlight: Investment banking fees and market activity improved.

JPM.title: Big banks brace for lower net interest income if rate cuts accelerate
JPM.description: A sector-focused piece examined how a faster path to rate cuts could pressure net interest margins across large banks, even as credit quality remains stable.
JPM.snippet: The article is relevant to banks broadly and notes that margin pressure could affect deposit-heavy lenders over the next few quarters.

highlights.highlight: Lower rates may compress bank net interest margins.

JPM.title: JPMorgan clears regulatory review tied to capital planning process
JPM.description: JPMorgan said its latest capital planning review concluded without material findings, supporting buyback flexibility and a stable capital return posture.
JPM.snippet: The update removed an overhang on capital actions and was viewed positively by analysts covering the stock.

XOM.title: Oil jumps after supply disruption raises concern over near-term crude availability
XOM.description: Crude prices moved higher after a shipping disruption tightened prompt barrels, improving the near-term earnings backdrop for large upstream producers.
XOM.snippet: Analysts said integrated oil majors with strong upstream exposure could benefit if benchmark crude remains elevated through the next several weeks.

highlights.highlight: Higher crude prices improve the backdrop for upstream earnings.

XOM.title: Exxon expands Guyana production guidance after stronger field performance
XOM.description: Exxon said output from its Guyana assets is tracking above plan, improving production visibility and supporting near-term cash generation.
XOM.snippet: The company pointed to stronger field reliability and expected production gains as a support for revenue.

highlights.highlight: Production visibility improved at key upstream assets.

XOM.title: Energy stocks rise with crude while airlines and chemicals lag
XOM.description: A broad market piece reviewed how energy shares outperformed on higher crude prices while fuel-sensitive sectors trailed.
XOM.snippet: The article was sector-focused and not specific to any one integrated major.

highlights.highlight: Energy shares outperformed with crude higher.
